name: Draft Editing & Delete
theme: Complete the write story — edit drafts and delete messages.

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <message-id>' command exists under a 'drafts' subcommand group, accepts a positional message-id argument, and succeeds (exit 0) when updating at least one property with mocked osascript
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --subject "New subject"' via CliRunner with mock_osascript configured to return success
Verify exit code is 0
Verify mock_osascript was invoked with at least one call
Verify the generated AppleScript targets the draft by its message ID
criteria.threshold: Command succeeds with exit 0 and the generated AppleScript modifies the targeted draft

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <id> --subject "New Subject"' generates AppleScript that sets the subject of the draft to the new value
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --subject "Updated meeting notes"' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it sets the subject property to 'Updated meeting notes'
criteria.threshold: Generated AppleScript sets the subject property to the specified value

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <id> --body "New body text"' generates AppleScript that replaces the draft's body content
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --body "Updated body content"' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it sets the content/body of the draft to 'Updated body content'
criteria.threshold: Generated AppleScript replaces the body content with the specified value

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <id> --body-file <path>' reads the file content and uses it as the new body, replacing the draft's existing body
testSteps: Create a temporary file with body content 'Body from file'
Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --body-file <tmpfile>' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it sets the body to 'Body from file'
criteria.threshold: Body content read from file and used in the generated AppleScript

criteria.description: Providing both --body and --body-file to 'mailctl drafts edit' produces a usage error and non-zero exit code (mutually exclusive body sources)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --body "text" --body-file somefile.txt' via CliRunner
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify stderr or output mentions that --body and --body-file are mutually exclusive
criteria.threshold: Mutually exclusive body sources fail fast with a clear error

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <id> --to <address>' replaces the draft's To recipients with the specified address(es). --to is repeatable for multiple recipients
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --to alice@example.com --to bob@example.com' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it clears existing To recipients and sets them to alice@example.com and bob@example.com
criteria.threshold: To recipients replaced with the specified addresses in the generated AppleScript

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <id> --cc <address>' replaces CC recipients and '--bcc <address>' replaces BCC recipients, both repeatable
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --cc cc@example.com' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify the generated AppleScript sets CC recipients to cc@example.com
Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --bcc bcc@example.com' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify the generated AppleScript sets BCC recipients to bcc@example.com
criteria.threshold: CC and BCC recipients correctly replaced in generated AppleScript

criteria.description: '--add-to <address>' adds a recipient to the existing To list without removing current recipients. '--remove-to <address>' removes a specific recipient from the To list
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --add-to new@example.com' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0 and the generated AppleScript creates a new to recipient without first clearing existing recipients
Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --remove-to old@example.com' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0 and the generated AppleScript removes the recipient with address old@example.com
criteria.threshold: --add-to appends without clearing, --remove-to removes a specific recipient

criteria.description: '--to' and '--add-to' are mutually exclusive — combining them in one invocation produces a usage error (--to replaces ALL, --add-to adds incrementally; mixing is ambiguous)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --to alice@example.com --add-to bob@example.com' via CliRunner
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify error message explains that --to and --add-to cannot be combined
criteria.threshold: Mutually exclusive --to and --add-to fail fast with a clear error

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <id> --attach <path>' adds an attachment to the draft. Path is validated for existence before generating AppleScript
testSteps: Create a temporary file at a known path
Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --attach <tmpfile>' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it adds the attachment using the file path
Run with a non-existent file path and verify exit code is non-zero with an error about the file not being found
criteria.threshold: Attachment added in AppleScript; non-existent path fails with a clear error before any AppleScript runs

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <id> --remove-attach <filename>' generates AppleScript that removes the named attachment from the draft
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --remove-attach "report.pdf"' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it targets the attachment named 'report.pdf' for removal
criteria.threshold: Generated AppleScript removes the specified attachment by name

criteria.description: Multiple edit options can be combined in a single invocation: '--subject "New" --body "Updated" --add-to extra@example.com' applies all changes in one AppleScript call
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --subject "New Subject" --body "New body" --add-to extra@example.com' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it sets the subject, replaces the body, AND adds the recipient
Verify only one osascript call was made (batched)
criteria.threshold: All changes applied in a single batched AppleScript invocation

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts edit <id>' with no edit options (no --subject, --body, --to, etc.) produces a usage error telling the user to specify at least one change
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345' (no edit options) via CliRunner
Verify exit code is non-zero
Verify stderr or output mentions that at least one edit option is required
criteria.threshold: No-options invocation fails with a clear error directing the user to specify a change

criteria.description: '--dry-run' on 'mailctl drafts edit' prints what WOULD be changed (message ID, which fields) without executing any AppleScript
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --subject "New" --body "Updated" --dry-run' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Verify stdout describes the intended changes (message ID, subject change, body change)
Verify mock_osascript was NOT called (zero osascript invocations)
criteria.threshold: --dry-run prints the planned changes and executes zero osascript calls

criteria.description: '--json' on 'mailctl drafts edit' emits structured JSON with at least: action ('edited'), message_id, and the fields that were changed
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts edit 12345 --subject "New Subject" --json' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Parse stdout as JSON
Verify it is a valid JSON object with keys: action (value 'edited'), message_id, and a representation of changes applied (e.g., subject field)
criteria.threshold: Valid JSON with required fields emitted for draft edit

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages delete <message-id>' command exists as a subcommand of 'messages', moves the message to Trash by default (non-destructive), and succeeds (exit 0) with mocked osascript
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345' via CliRunner with mock_osascript configured to return success
Verify exit code is 0
Verify mock_osascript was invoked with at least one call
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it moves the message to the Trash mailbox (NOT permanent deletion)
criteria.threshold: Default delete moves to Trash; generated AppleScript performs a move, not a permanent delete

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages delete <id> --permanent' generates AppleScript that permanently deletes the message. Without --yes, the command prompts for confirmation (y/N default N)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --permanent' via CliRunner with input 'y' and mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0 when user confirms
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it permanently deletes the message (uses 'delete' verb, not move to Trash)
Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --permanent' via CliRunner with input 'n' (or empty input for default N)
Verify exit code is 0 (aborted gracefully) and mock_osascript was NOT called
criteria.threshold: --permanent deletes permanently on confirmation; aborts without AppleScript on rejection; defaults to N

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages delete <id> --permanent --yes' skips the confirmation prompt and proceeds directly to permanent deletion
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --permanent --yes' via CliRunner with mock_osascript (no stdin input)
Verify exit code is 0
Verify mock_osascript was invoked
Verify no confirmation prompt was printed to stdout
criteria.threshold: --yes with --permanent skips the prompt and executes the permanent deletion

criteria.description: '--yes' without '--permanent' has no effect — default delete (move to Trash) does not require or show a confirmation prompt
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --yes' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Verify the operation moved to Trash (not permanent delete)
Verify no confirmation prompt was shown
criteria.threshold: --yes alone does not change delete behaviour (still moves to Trash, no prompt)

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages delete' accepts multiple message IDs and processes all of them in a batched AppleScript call
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages delete 100 200 300' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify all three message IDs (100, 200, 300) are referenced
Verify the operation is batched into a minimal number of osascript calls (ideally one)
criteria.threshold: Multiple IDs accepted and all processed; batched into one osascript call

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages delete <id> --account <name>' scopes the delete AppleScript to the specified account
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --account "Work"' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0
Inspect the generated AppleScript and verify it references account "Work" when locating the message to delete
criteria.threshold: AppleScript scoped to the specified account when --account is provided

criteria.description: '--dry-run' on 'mailctl messages delete' prints what WOULD be deleted (message IDs, Trash vs permanent) without executing any AppleScript
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --dry-run' via CliRunner with mock_osascript
Verify exit code is 0 and stdout describes moving message 12345 to Trash
Verify mock_osascript was NOT called
Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --permanent --dry-run' via CliRunner
Verify stdout describes permanently deleting message 12345
Verify mock_osascript was NOT called
criteria.threshold: --dry-run prints the planned deletion (including Trash vs permanent) and executes zero osascript calls

criteria.description: Delete produces human-readable confirmation output on success and '--json' emits structured JSON with action ('deleted' or 'trashed'), message_ids, and permanent flag
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345' and verify stdout contains confirmation mentioning the message was moved to Trash
Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --json' with mock_osascript
Parse stdout as JSON and verify it is an object with keys: action ('trashed'), message_ids (list), permanent (false)
Run 'mailctl messages delete 12345 --permanent --yes --json' with mock_osascript
Parse stdout as JSON and verify action is 'deleted' and permanent is true
criteria.threshold: Human-readable confirmation and valid JSON with required fields for both Trash and permanent delete

criteria.description: Delete and drafts edit with a nonexistent message ID (simulated via mock error) produce clear errors mentioning the message was not found, with non-zero exit code
testSteps: Configure mock_osascript to simulate a script error when processing message ID 99999
Run 'mailctl messages delete 99999'
Verify exit code is non-zero and stderr mentions the error
Run 'mailctl drafts edit 99999 --subject "Test"'
Verify exit code is non-zero and stderr mentions the error
criteria.threshold: Nonexistent message ID produces non-zero exit and actionable error on stderr for both commands

criteria.description: 'mailctl drafts --help' lists 'edit' as a subcommand, and 'mailctl drafts edit --help' describes the positional MESSAGE_ID argument and all options (--subject, --body, --body-file, --to, --cc, --bcc, --add-to, --remove-to, --attach, --remove-attach, --dry-run, --json)
testSteps: Run 'mailctl drafts --help' via CliRunner and verify 'edit' is listed as a subcommand
Run 'mailctl drafts edit --help' via CliRunner
Verify help mentions: MESSAGE_ID (positional), --subject, --body, --body-file, --to, --cc, --bcc, --add-to, --remove-to, --attach, --remove-attach, --dry-run, --json
criteria.threshold: 'drafts' group visible with 'edit' subcommand; all options documented in help

criteria.description: 'mailctl messages --help' lists 'delete' as a subcommand alongside existing 'list', 'show', 'search', 'mark', 'move'. 'mailctl messages delete --help' describes MESSAGE_IDS, --permanent, --yes, --account, --dry-run, --json
testSteps: Run 'mailctl messages --help' via CliRunner
Verify 'delete' is listed alongside 'list', 'show', 'search', 'mark', 'move'
Run 'mailctl messages delete --help' via CliRunner
Verify help mentions: MESSAGE_IDS (positional, multiple), --permanent, --yes, --account, --dry-run, --json
criteria.threshold: 'delete' visible in messages help alongside all prior subcommands; all options documented

criteria.description: Draft edit and delete implementations follow the established build/perform/register pattern: AppleScript in build_*_script() functions, thin Typer handlers, shared output/error-handling utilities reused from prior sprints
testSteps: Inspect the draft edit implementation and verify build_*_script() functions construct the AppleScript
Inspect the delete implementation and verify build_*_script() functions construct the AppleScript
Verify command handlers use handle_mail_error from mailctl.output for error handling
Verify render_output or render_error is used for output, not ad-hoc print statements for structured data
Verify the code reuses existing patterns (e.g. _escape_applescript_string for string escaping, run_applescript from engine)
criteria.threshold: Both features follow the established architecture with code reuse from prior sprints

criteria.description: Comprehensive unit tests exist for draft editing covering: --subject, --body, --body-file, mutually-exclusive body sources, --to, --cc, --bcc, --add-to, --remove-to, --to/--add-to conflict, --attach, --remove-attach, combined changes, no-options error, --dry-run, --json, error handling
testSteps: Inspect tests/unit/ for draft edit test file(s)

Verify tests cover at minimum: --subject, --body, --body-file, body conflict error, --to (replace), --cc, --bcc, --add-to, --remove-to, --to + --add-to conflict, --attach, --remove-attach, combined changes, no-options error, --dry-run, --json, AppleScript error handling
Run the draft edit tests and verify they all pass
criteria.threshold: At least 16 draft edit test cases covering the above scenarios, all passing

criteria.description: Comprehensive unit tests exist for delete covering: single ID to Trash, --permanent with confirmation yes, --permanent with confirmation no (abort), --permanent --yes (skip prompt), --yes without --permanent, bulk IDs, --account scoping, --dry-run (Trash and permanent), --json (Trash and permanent), error handling
testSteps: Inspect tests/unit/ for delete test file(s)

Verify tests cover at minimum: default Trash, --permanent + y, --permanent + n, --permanent --yes, --yes alone, bulk 3+ IDs, --account, --dry-run default, --dry-run --permanent, --json Trash, --json permanent, AppleScript error handling
Run the delete tests and verify they all pass
criteria.threshold: At least 12 delete test cases covering the above scenarios, all passing

criteria.description: Full test suite (pytest from project root) passes with zero failures, including all Sprint 1-7 tests and new Sprint 8 tests
testSteps: Run 'pytest -v' from the project root
Verify all tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors)
Verify Sprint 1-7 tests still pass (regression)
criteria.threshold: All tests pass, exit code 0

criteria.description: All Sprint 1-7 commands still work: '--version', accounts list, mailboxes list, messages list/show/search/mark/move, compose, reply, forward — with unchanged options and outputs
testSteps: Run 'mailctl --version' and verify version output
Run 'mailctl --help' and verify all command groups are listed including accounts, mailboxes, messages, compose, reply, forward, and the new drafts group
Run the full Sprint 1-7 unit test suite and verify all tests pass
criteria.threshold: All Sprint 1-7 functionality preserved; no test regressions

outOfScope: Sending from drafts edit — editing a draft does not send it. The user must separately run 'mailctl compose' or use the draft-first workflow; there is no --dangerously-send on the edit command
Draft listing — listing all drafts in the Drafts mailbox is already possible via 'mailctl messages list --mailbox Drafts'; no dedicated 'mailctl drafts list' command in this sprint
Draft creation via 'mailctl drafts' — draft creation is handled by 'mailctl compose' (Sprint 5); this sprint only adds edit
Undo/revert for delete — no mechanism to undo a delete or restore from Trash in v1
Delete across accounts — deleting a message by ID without --account when the ID is ambiguous across accounts is not handled; user must specify --account
Permanent delete of multiple messages with per-message confirmation — bulk --permanent uses a single confirmation for the entire batch, not one per message

safetyNotes.noSendInEdit: The 'mailctl drafts edit' command MUST NOT include a send path. There is no --dangerously-send option on the edit command. Editing a draft only modifies its content; it never sends. If the generated AppleScript contains a 'send' verb, that is a bug.
safetyNotes.deleteIsMoveByDefault: Default delete moves to Trash, which is non-destructive and reversible. Only --permanent actually deletes. This makes delete safe in automation pipelines.
safetyNotes.permanentDeleteConfirmation: The --permanent flag requires interactive confirmation (y/N, default N) unless --yes is also provided. This matches the safety pattern from compose's --dangerously-send confirmation.
safetyNotes.evalTestingSafety: The evaluator MAY run 'mailctl messages delete' against real Mail.app ONLY to move messages to Trash (never --permanent). The evaluator MUST restore moved messages after testing. Per CLAUDE.md, the evaluator MUST NOT permanently delete messages from the user's real accounts.
safetyNotes.noRealDeletion: Under no circumstances should the evaluator or generator invoke 'mailctl messages delete --permanent' against real Mail.app. All permanent delete testing MUST use mocked osascript.
